source.sourcePolicy: Rules and flow are compiled only from the jurisdiction reference/source documents supplied in the Nationwide archive, plus the user-supplied Nebraska reference.

questions.prompt: Are you asking about your own record?
options: I am helping someone complete their own check

questions.prompt: Is this a state or local matter from Kentucky, rather than a federal matter?
How did the Kentucky case or record end?
options: Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted
Juvenile adjudication or offense committed as a minor

questions.prompt: What level or type was the charge?
Do any of these Kentucky descriptions sound close to your situation?
options: Misdemeanor, violation, or traffic-infraction conviction expungement under KRS 431.078
Eligible felony conviction vacatur and expungement under KRS 431.073
Acquittal, dismissal, or failure-to-indict expungement under KRS 431.076
Juvenile automatic expungement on qualifying dismissal
Juvenile petition to vacate and expunge under KRS 610.330
None of these / I am not sure
questions.helper: This answer is optional and does not select your legal pathway. The backend uses the factual answers and state rules.

questions.prompt: How old were you when the offense or case began?
Have you completed every part of the sentence, supervision, probation, parole, classes, or community service?
Have you received a pardon, certificate of rehabilitation, or similar executive relief for this conviction?
What Kentucky county, parish, borough, district, or local filing area handled the case?
Which court or agency handled the case?
What charge, statute, or record description appears on the record?
Do you have court paperwork or a criminal-history record showing the exact charge and outcome?
Where in Kentucky was the case handled?
What case, docket, cause, or arrest number appears on the record?

pathways.label: Misdemeanor, violation, or traffic-infraction conviction expungement under KRS 431.078
pathways.summary: The offense cannot be a sex offense or an offense committed against a child. The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction in the five years before filing and no felony or misdemeanor proceeding pending or being instituted. The offense cannot remain subject to enhancement for a later offense, unless the enhancement period has expired. The petition is filed no sooner than five years after sentence completion or successful completion of probation, whichever is later, except the named…
ruleClauses: The offense cannot be a sex offense or an offense committed against a child.
The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction in the five years before filing and no felony or misdemeanor proceeding pending or being instituted.
The offense cannot remain subject to enhancement for a later offense, unless the enhancement period has expired.
The petition is filed no sooner than five years after sentence completion or successful completion of probation, whichever is later, except the named voided first-possession routes.
The source requires an eligibility certification, a separate petition for each case, notarized or clerk-witnessed signature, victim/other-person notice, and a hearing.
waitingRules: The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction in the five years before filing and no felony or misdemeanor proceeding pending or being instituted.
The petition is filed no sooner than five years after sentence completion or successful completion of probation, whichever is later, except the named voided first-possession routes.
exclusionRules: The offense cannot be a sex offense or an offense committed against a child.
The offense cannot remain subject to enhancement for a later offense, unless the enhancement period has expired.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Eligible felony conviction vacatur and expungement under KRS 431.073
pathways.summary: The offense must be on an eligible statutory list, be an eligible series from one incident, qualify under the additional subsection stated in the form, or have a full gubernatorial pardon attached. The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction during the five years before filing and no pending felony or misdemeanor proceeding. The application is filed no sooner than five years after sentence, probation, or parole completion, whichever is later. The form collects rehabilitation,…
ruleClauses: The offense must be on an eligible statutory list, be an eligible series from one incident, qualify under the additional subsection stated in the form, or have a full gubernatorial pardon attached.
The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction during the five years before filing and no pending felony or misdemeanor proceeding.
The application is filed no sooner than five years after sentence, probation, or parole completion, whichever is later.
The form collects rehabilitation, law-abiding conduct, life impact, and other discretionary facts for the subsection (1)(d) route.
The source requires eligibility certification, prosecutor response within 60 days, hearing no later than 120 days, victim notice, and final payment before completion.
waitingRules: The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction during the five years before filing and no pending felony or misdemeanor proceeding.
The application is filed no sooner than five years after sentence, probation, or parole completion, whichever is later.
The source requires eligibility certification, prosecutor response within 60 days, hearing no later than 120 days, victim notice, and final payment before completion.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Acquittal, dismissal, or failure-to-indict expungement under KRS 431.076
pathways.summary: Acquittal or dismissal with prejudice requires at least 60 days before filing and cannot have been exchanged for a guilty plea to another offense. Felony dismissal without prejudice requires at least three years; misdemeanor dismissal without prejudice requires at least one year. A felony held to the grand jury but not indicted or charged by information uses the six-month route stated in the form. A separate petition is required for each case, all charges must be listed, agencies holding…
ruleClauses: Acquittal or dismissal with prejudice requires at least 60 days before filing and cannot have been exchanged for a guilty plea to another offense.
Felony dismissal without prejudice requires at least three years; misdemeanor dismissal without prejudice requires at least one year.
A felony held to the grand jury but not indicted or charged by information uses the six-month route stated in the form.
A separate petition is required for each case, all charges must be listed, agencies holding records must be identified, and the signature must be notarized or witnessed by the clerk.
waitingRules: Acquittal or dismissal with prejudice requires at least 60 days before filing and cannot have been exchanged for a guilty plea to another offense.
Felony dismissal without prejudice requires at least three years; misdemeanor dismissal without prejudice requires at least one year.
exclusionRules: Acquittal or dismissal with prejudice requires at least 60 days before filing and cannot have been exchanged for a guilty plea to another offense.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Juvenile automatic expungement on qualifying dismissal
pathways.summary: The juvenile order states that specified dismissal situations require expungement by law without a petition, motion, or other action by the child. The frontend should present verification guidance rather than a paid petition when the automatic-dismissal facts are confirmed.
ruleClauses: The juvenile order states that specified dismissal situations require expungement by law without a petition, motion, or other action by the child.
The frontend should present verification guidance rather than a paid petition when the automatic-dismissal facts are confirmed.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Juvenile petition to vacate and expunge under KRS 610.330
pathways.summary: The petition applies when the juvenile record was not already handled by the automatic dismissal order. The source includes two-year timing conditions tied to case closure or unconditional release and collects rehabilitation facts. The court sets a hearing, gives the county attorney an opportunity to respond, and may grant or deny based on statutory eligibility.
ruleClauses: The petition applies when the juvenile record was not already handled by the automatic dismissal order.
The source includes two-year timing conditions tied to case closure or unconditional release and collects rehabilitation facts.
The court sets a hearing, gives the county attorney an opportunity to respond, and may grant or deny based on statutory eligibility.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

The petition is filed no sooner than five years after sentence completion or successful completion of probation, whichever is later, except the named voided first-possession routes.

Acquittal or dismissal with prejudice requires at least 60 days before filing and cannot have been exchanged for a guilty plea to another offense.
Felony dismissal without prejudice requires at least three years; misdemeanor dismissal without prejudice requires at least one year.
The frontend should present verification guidance rather than a paid petition when the automatic-dismissal facts are confirmed.
The petition applies when the juvenile record was not already handled by the automatic dismissal order.
The offense cannot be a sex offense or an offense committed against a child.
The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction in the five years before filing and no felony or misdemeanor proceeding pending or being instituted.
The offense cannot remain subject to enhancement for a later offense, unless the enhancement period has expired.
The source requires an eligibility certification, a separate petition for each case, notarized or clerk-witnessed signature, victim/other-person notice, and a hearing.
The offense must be on an eligible statutory list, be an eligible series from one incident, qualify under the additional subsection stated in the form, or have a full gubernatorial pardon attached.
The person must have no felony or misdemeanor conviction during the five years before filing and no pending felony or misdemeanor proceeding.
The source requires eligibility certification, prosecutor response within 60 days, hearing no later than 120 days, victim notice, and final payment before completion.
A felony held to the grand jury but not indicted or charged by information uses the six-month route stated in the form.
A separate petition is required for each case, all charges must be listed, agencies holding records must be identified, and the signature must be notarized or witnessed by the clerk.
The juvenile order states that specified dismissal situations require expungement by law without a petition, motion, or other action by the child.

generatorSelectionContract.failureBehavior: Return needs_more_info or needs_review. Never fall back to a generic packet or another jurisdiction generator.

packet_ready.title: You may have a packet-ready path.

packet_ready_with_caution.title: You may have a path, with cautions.

needs_more_info.title: We need a few more details.

guidance_only.title: We can give you state-specific next steps.

not_covered_yet.title: This record type is not supported by the current packet set.

likely_not_eligible.title: This record may not match a self-help filing path.

needs_review.title: This situation needs review before a packet is generated.

hard_stop.title: This tool cannot prepare a packet for this matter.

copyGuardrails: Use Kentucky-specific remedy terminology returned by the profile; do not relabel every remedy as expungement.
Use “may qualify,” “possible path,” and “the court or agency makes the final decision.”
Do not promise erasure, sealing, eligibility, timing, filing acceptance, or a court outcome.
Do not show checkout unless the backend returns a packet-ready result and paymentAllowed=true.

sourceSections.title: The above-stated offense(s) is/are eligible to be vacated and expunged as follows: (check only one)
sourceSections.text:
q the offense is one of the eligible offenses listed in KRS 431.073(1)(a).
q the offenses are a series of eligible offenses listed in KRS 431.073(1)(a) which arose from a single incident.
q a full pardon has been granted by the Governor, a copy of which is attached.
3. The Defendant has not, in the five years prior to the filing of this Application, been convicted of a felony or
misdemeanor.
4. No proceeding concerning a felony or misdemeanor is pending or being instituted against the Defendant.
5. This Application is filed no sooner than five years after completion of the Defendant’s sentence or successful
completion of the Defendant’s probation or parole, whichever occurs later.

AOC-496.3
Rev. 6-23

The Defendant moves that this Application to Vacate and Expunge a Felony Conviction be granted and that this Court enter
an Order that the Kentucky State Police, the Kentucky Department of Libraries and Archives, and the following agencies
expunge any records in the agencies' custody regarding these charges: LIST AGENCIES AND ADDRESSES HERE:
(Records may be held at multiple agencies. Please identify any government agency that may have a record of
your conviction such as, but not limited to, jail facilities or arresting agencies.)
_______________________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________________

AOC-496.3
Rev. 6-23

I hereby state that the information provided above is true and accurate to the best of my knowledge.
Note: Defendant/Applicant must sign this Application in the presence of a notary or the circuit court clerk so that the
notary/clerk can witness his/her signature.

______________________________, 2_______ ____________________________________________
Date Defendant/Applicant Signature

Subscribed and sworn to before me by _____________________________________________ this ________ day of
____________________________, 2________.
_____________________________________________
Notary/Clerk

My Commission Expires: _________________________ By: _______________________________________ D.C.

There is a $50 non-refundable filing fee per application, due at the time of filing. The clerk cannot take your
application without proper payment of this fee.
If an expungement order is granted, you will be charged an additional fee of $250 (“expungement fee”), which
you may pay in installments. If you would like to ask the Court to establish an installment payment plan, you will
need to fill out the request below. Please note that the expungement cannot be completed until payment in full
is received.
A copy of your current expungement eligibility certification must be attached to this Application.

REQUEST FOR INSTALLMENT PAYMENT PLAN

q The Defendant requests that the Court establish an installment plan for the payment of the expungement fee of $250.
Defendant requests to pay $ ____________ q weekly q every other week q twice per month q monthly
q other _________________________________________________________________________ , until paid in full.

NOTICE TO COMMONWEALTH/COUNTY ATTORNEY

Pursuant to KRS 431.073(2), the office of the Commonwealth Attorney or County Attorney who prosecuted the case shall
file a response to this Application within 60 days after being served with this notice. An extension may be granted for good
cause, but a hearing on the Application shall occur no later than 120 days following the filing of the Application. The office
of the Commonwealth or County Attorney shall notify the victim of the crime if there was an identified victim.

FOR CLERK USE ONLY

This Application to Vacate and Expunge a Felony Conviction was sent on the ______ day of ______________________,
______, to the Commonwealth or County Attorney who prosecuted the case and the County Attorney of the County where
the Judgment was entered.

_____________________________________________
Clerk

By: _______________________________________ D.C.

In support of this Petition, the Defendant states as follows: (check all that apply)
 At least sixty (60) days prior to filing this Petition, the Defendant was acquitted of the above-listed charge(s) and the
charge(s) was/were not expunged pursuant to KRS 431.076(1)(a);

 At least sixty (60) days prior to filing this Petition, the above-listed charge(s) was/were dismissed with prejudice
and said dismissal was NOT in exchange for a guilty plea to another offense and the charge(s) was/were not expunged
pursuant to KRS 431.076(1)(a);
 For felony charges, at least three (3) years prior to the filing of this Petition, the above-listed charge(s) was/were
dismissed without prejudice and said dismissal was NOT in exchange for a guilty plea to another offense;

 For misdemeanor charges, at least one (1) year prior to the filing of this Petition, the above-listed charge(s) was/were
dismissed without prejudice and said dismissal was NOT in exchange for a guilty plea to another offense; AND

 The above-listed felony charge(s) originally filed in District Court has/have not resulted in an indictment by the grand
jury or in an information filed by the Commonwealth’s Attorney, and six (6) months have elapsed since
the date of the District Court decision to hold the matter to the grand jury.

Complete either Section I. or II., whichever is applicable.
I. Check one
q Dismissal for lack of jurisdiction (mandatory diversion). The Court having found the above-named child eligible
for mandatory diversion, pursuant to KRS 635.010(4), JCRPP 2E., and JCRPP 12D., and having dismissed the above
referenced case, it is hereby ORDERED that:
OR
q Dismissal at first appearance with intent to reinitiate original complaint and divert. The Court having dismissed the
above-referenced case pursuant to the Commonwealth’s motion to dismiss with the intent to reinitiate the original complaint
with the Court Designated Worker for diversion, pursuant to JCRPP 6D. and JCRPP 12D., it is hereby ORDERED that:

(1) All records in the custody of the Court concerning the following offense(s):

OFFENSE: ________________________________ OFFENSE: ________________________________
OFFENSE: ________________________________ OFFENSE: ________________________________
OFFENSE: ________________________________ OFFENSE: ________________________________
included in __________________________ (case number) are hereby expunged upon entry of this Order.

(2) Upon entry of this Order to expunge the above-specified records, the proceedings in the case shall be deemed never
to have occurred, all index references shall be deleted, and the person and Court may properly reply that no record exists
with respect to such offense upon any inquiry in the matter.

(3) The person whose record is expunged shall not have to disclose the fact of the record or any matter relating thereto
on an application for employment, credit, or other type of application.

(4) Inspection of the records included in this Order may hereafter be permitted by the Court only upon Petition by the
person who is the subject of such records, and only to those persons named in such Petition.

(5) The Court Designated Worker shall proceed with the diversion agreement process on the original complaint.

____________________________________, 2______ __________________________________________
Date Judge

IN THE INTEREST OF: ____________________________________________________________________, A CHILD

DOB Sex Race SSN

FINDINGS OF FACT
The AOC-JV-30, Petition to Vacate and Expunge Juvenile Record, having been filed in this Court setting forth facts which
request this Court to vacate the adjudication of the below-named offense(s) and to expunge the records of the person
named in the Petition (hereinafter referred to as “the child”), notice having been given, a hearing having been held, and
the Court having been fully advised FINDS:
